news.title: Mortgage approvals in UK rise to over 63,500: Bank of England data
news.para: British lenders approved the most mortgages in four months in March, with consumer lending growing at its fastest annual rate in over two years. Despite a drop in consumer confidence and concerns over rising mortgage rates, house prices continued to rise modestly in April. Economists anticipate potential interest rate hikes, which could impact future house price inflation.

news.title: Nod for construction in Goa's developable area on 'need-based' basis only: Minister
news.para: Goa's Town and Country Planning Minister Vishwajit Rane announced that construction permits will now be granted on a "need-based" basis only in developable areas. This decision comes after the state initiated steps to freeze land in eco-sensitive zones, including riverbanks, aiming to rectify past planning errors and protect ecologically sensitive regions.

news.title: Net leasing of office spaces in NCR down 60% in Jan-Mar to 1.5 million sq ft: JLL
news.para: Delhi-NCR's office market experienced a significant slowdown in the first quarter, with net leasing plummeting by 60% to 1.5 million sq ft due to reduced new supply. Despite this, JLL India maintains that long-term fundamentals remain strong, anticipating a rebound in leasing activity in the coming quarters.

news.title: Union government plans to integrate construction labour cess into social security system
news.para: Union minister Shobha Karandlaje has alleged that some state governments, including Karnataka, have misused funds collected for labour welfare. She announced the central government's plan to integrate this labour cess into the formal social security system. This will ensure the money directly provides pensions and ESIC healthcare benefits to construction labourers.

news.title: Ahmedabad: Real estate partners booked for ₹11.3 crore fraud
news.para: A businessman has filed a complaint alleging a Rs 11.3 crore fraud at a real estate firm. He accuses two managing partners of diverting Rs 2.25 crore to personal accounts and Rs 5.5 crore to associates, while also illegally selling nine flats worth Rs 3.5 crore. The accused allegedly misused firm documents for personal gain.

news.title: Pidilite Ventures to exit Buildnext via share swap with JSW One Platforms
news.para: Pidilite Industries' wholly-owned subsidiary Pidilite Ventures has entered into a share swap agreement with JSW One Platforms, under which it will transfer its stake in home design and project management firm Buildnext to JSW One in exchange for shares of the latter, while simultaneously investing up to ₹22 crore more in Buildnext through convertible preference shares.

news.title: Ludhiana improvement trust eyes ₹197 crore in auction for 18-year-abandoned ruin
news.para: Ludhiana Improvement Trust is attempting to sell a derelict 2.2-acre multi-storey building for ₹197 crore after an 18-year vacancy. The 77-unit complex, built for commercial and residential use, has been a symbol of administrative inertia, with past reuse proposals failing. Officials hope the current buoyant commercial real estate market will secure a buyer.

news.title: Chandigarh administration fast-tracking one-time relief policy
news.para: Chandigarh administration is fast-tracking a "one-time relief" policy to regularise internal modifications in thousands of CHB dwellings. This pragmatic solution aims to provide homeowners a clean slate for internal changes and boundary wall adjustments, provided there is no encroachment on public land. The policy seeks a permanent resolution, offering residents peace of mind while maintaining structural integrity.

news.title: Ghaziabad: GDA sanctions ₹100 crore for cranes with 100 meter reach after Indirapuram fire
news.para: Following a recent fire incident, Ghaziabad authorities are investing Rs 100 crore in two new 102-meter hydraulic platforms to combat fires in the city's rapidly growing high-rise buildings. This move addresses the inadequacy of current firefighting equipment, which can only reach 42 meters, as height restrictions for many building types have been removed, paving the way for further vertical expansion.

news.title: Over 25 lakh properties in Bengaluru brought under e-Khata system: Karnataka deputy CM
news.para: Bengaluru is witnessing a major digital transformation in property records. Over 25 lakh properties are now part of the e-Khata system, ensuring accuracy and transparency. The government is integrating Aadhaar, GPS, and digital documents for a robust database. Special camps are being organized to help citizens obtain their e-Khatas.

news.title: Delhi: 15 apply for ownership rights in unauthorised colonies; none yet for house regularisation
news.para: The SWAGAM portal, launched by the Municipal Corporation of Delhi, has recorded its first 15 applications for property ownership under the PM-UDAY scheme, but none have been submitted for regularisation yet. To boost participation, the MCD is actively engaging in awareness campaigns aimed at residents.

news.title: Bank, real estate, corporate scams have declined in India: ED director
news.para: Financial crimes like bank and real estate frauds are declining. The Enforcement Directorate (ED) is now focusing on cryptocurrency fraud, cyber-enabled crimes, terror financing, and narcotics trafficking. The agency filed significantly more charge sheets last fiscal year. ED's conviction rate stands at 94 percent. Assets worth over Rs 81,000 crore were attached.

news.title: Noida International Airport to begin commercial operations from June 15
news.para: Noida International Airport (NIA) in Jewar will commence commercial flight operations on June 15, 2026, with IndiGo operating the inaugural flight. Akasa Air and Air India Express are slated to follow soon after. The airport, developed by YIAPL, will initially handle 1.2 crore passengers annually, with plans for significant expansion.

news.title: Telangana HC pulls up HYDRAA over illegal construction in Salkam Cheruvu FTL area
news.para: The Telangana High Court has sharply questioned HYDRAA's failure to act against the Barrister Fatima Owaisi Educational Campus. The institution is allegedly built in the Full Tank Level area of Salkam Cheruvu without proper approvals. The court ordered a detailed inquiry into officials who neglected their duties. Students' safety was also a concern raised by the judge.

news.title: Goa's town planning department proposes to tag 28.4 lakh sq meter as ‘non-developable’
news.para: Goa's Town and Country Planning department is proposing to designate vast land tracts as non-developable. This move aims to protect significant natural areas. Three major parcels in Bardez and Pernem, covering over 28 lakh square meters, are included. These proposals are now open for public objections. This marks a shift from previous land use policies.

news.title: House prices in UK rise in April despite Iran war headwinds: Nationwide
news.para: UK house prices have surprised economists by rising for the fourth consecutive month in April. This upward trend continues despite global uncertainties and a dip in consumer confidence. Strong household finances and income growth are supporting the housing market. Nationwide Building Society reported a 0.4% increase in April, with prices now 3.0% higher year-on-year.

news.title: The Indian Hotels Company's amã Stays & Trails enters Andaman & Nicobar Islands
news.para: The Indian Hotels Company (IHCL) has expanded its homestay portfolio to the Andaman and Nicobar Islands with a 10-bungalow cluster in Port Blair. Separately, the company concluded a ₹222-crore investment to acquire a majority stake in Brij Hospital.

news.title: Kolhapur civic body’s survey finds 204 illegal constructions near Tawde hotel, 88 in contempt of court
news.para: A Kolhapur Municipal Corporation survey revealed 204 illegal constructions on 158 acres of civic land near Tawde hotel, with 88 violating court orders. Thirty-three structures are within the Panchaganga river's flood line, posing environmental and safety risks. The survey followed a Supreme Court ruling confirming KMC's ownership of the land.

news.title: EFC (I) to raise ₹160 crore via rights issue at ₹150 per share
news.para: Managed workspace operator EFC (I) Limited has finalised terms for a rights issue of over 1.06 crore equity shares at ₹150 apiece, and separately incorporated a wholly-owned subsidiary to hold commercial property in Pune's Shivaji Nagar.
